title: Reusable Client Architecture, Transport Abstractions, Middleware, and Configuration · Go Client Systems · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A platform SDK team integrating a staging control plane must build a first protocol model from narrated fixtures and explain each policy boundary for reusable client architecture, transport abstractions, middleware, and configuration, producing small composable client library boundary. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a Go 1.26 net/http small composable client library boundary while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory…
objectives: Centralize base authority, transport, timeout, user agent, credentials, limits, and defaults in an explicit client configuration. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Inject the narrow request execution capability needed for deterministic tests without cloning the whole HTTP library. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Compose authentication, retries, metrics, tracing, caching, and redaction with documented ordering and ownership. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Client Configuration
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Go staging control-plane client library needs base authority, injected transport, auth, retry, cache, metrics, redaction, and typed operation G-3. Order wrappers, separate per-request state, validate configuration, and keep protocol plumbing private. Competency probe: Centralize base authority, transport, timeout, user agent, credentials, limits, and defaults in an explicit client configuration. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Client Configuration.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for centralize base authority, transport, timeout, user agent, credentials, limits, and defaults in an explicit client configuration. demonstrate the result through go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, roundtripper, and httptest evidence. For Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Client Configuration, keep Client Configuration connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
